title: Phase Signal File
description: Schema for ~/catalyst/execution-core/workers/{TICKET}/phase-{name}.json — the per-phase coordination file written by the execution-core daemon and updated by phase-agent workers. The daemon dispatches work by writing this file; the phase-agent worker updates it (status, bg_job_id) as it runs; the daemon reads it to determine when to advance to the next phase.

ticket.description: Linear ticket identifier (e.g. 'CTL-123'). Matches the worker directory name.

phase.description: Name of the pipeline phase this signal file represents.

model.description: Claude model string passed to the phase-agent worker at dispatch time (e.g. 'opus', 'sonnet'). Determined by resolving Layer-1 and Layer-2 phaseAgents.models config.

turnCap.description: Maximum number of agent turns allowed for this phase invocation. When reached, the phase-agent emits status 'turn-cap-exhausted' and halts.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of this phase, written by the daemon on dispatch and updated by the phase-agent as work progresses.

bg_job_id.description: 8-character hex identifier of the Claude Code bg job running this phase. Written by the phase-agent at startup. Used for orphan detection and revive deduplication. null before the worker writes it or after a terminal status.

worktreePath.description: Absolute filesystem path to the git worktree created for this ticket/phase. null for phases that do not create a worktree (e.g. monitor-merge, monitor-deploy on some configurations).

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the daemon dispatched this phase (i.e. when the signal file was first written).

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last write to this signal file. The orphan reaper compares this against the current time to detect stalled workers.

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the phase reached a terminal status (done, failed, skipped, turn-cap-exhausted). null while still in progress.

failureReason.description: Short description of why the phase failed, written by phase-agent-emit-complete when --status failed is passed. Omit (null) on success — do not pass --reason to emit-complete on successful phases.

attentionReason.description: Human-readable explanation written when the daemon escalates the ticket to needs-human status. null during normal operation.

handoffPath.description: Absolute path to a phase artifact (e.g. research doc, plan doc, verify.json) passed to downstream phases as context. Written by the phase-agent at completion.

catalystSessionId.description: Catalyst session identifier (not the Claude Code session UUID). Used by the session tracker to correlate cost and duration metrics in catalyst.db.

pr.description: Pull request metadata written by the pr phase-agent after opening a PR. Only present on phase-pr signal files.

phaseTimestamps.description: Map of status values to ISO 8601 timestamps, recording when each status transition occurred. Useful for measuring phase duration.
